content: # Workbench Browser Proofshot QA¥n¥nUse this skill for browser-based verification, Playwright checks, proof screenshots, local web app dogfooding, and reproducible UI evidence.¥n¥n## Browser Verification Loop¥n¥n1. Start or identify the real app URL.¥n2. Record browser, viewport, account/auth state, and build/commit when available.¥n3. Exercise the user workflow, not only page load.¥n4. Capture screenshots or trace artifacts for states that matter.¥n5. Check console errors, failed network requests, obvious layout breakage, and final persisted state.¥n6. Report exact repro steps and evidence paths.¥n¥n## Boundaries¥n¥n- Do not edit code during report-only QA unless explicitly assigned.¥n- Do not treat a static screenshot as proof that a workflow works.¥n- Do not rely on cached authenticated state without saying so.¥n- Do not include private data in screenshots or copied logs.¥n¥n## Output Contract¥n¥nReturn:¥n¥n- `BROWSER_VERDICT`: PASS / FLAG / BLOCK.¥n- `RUN`: URL, viewport, browser/tool, commit/build if known.¥n- `STEPS`: concise repro or verification path.¥n- `EVIDENCE`: screenshot/trace/log paths.¥n- `FINDINGS`: ordered by severity.¥n- `FOLLOW-UP`: smallest fix or re-test needed.
description: Browser-based verification with screenshot, trace, console, and repro evidence.
name: workbench-browser-proofshot-qa

content: # Workbench Code Review¥n¥nUse this skill for reviewing code, diffs, task plans, live workflow changes, and implementation evidence before merge or closure.¥n¥n## Review Stance¥n¥n- Findings first. Do not lead with praise or a broad summary.¥n- Order findings by severity and user impact.¥n- Tie every finding to a file, command, issue/comment ID, run ID, or observable behavior.¥n- Prefer concrete repro or blast-radius evidence over style opinions.¥n- If no issues are found, say that clearly and name remaining test gaps.¥n¥n## Severity¥n¥n- `P0`: security, data loss, irreversible action, or broken primary workflow.¥n- `P1`: likely user-facing regression or unsafe automation.¥n- `P2`: correctness gap, missing edge case, or weak verification.¥n- `P3`: maintainability, clarity, or low-risk cleanup.¥n¥n## Checklist¥n¥n- Does the implementation match the promised objective?¥n- Are owner boundaries preserved?¥n- Are live mutations backed up and reversible?¥n- Are commands, IDs, and paths reproducible?¥n- Are tests or real-path checks sufficient for the blast radius?¥n- Did the change avoid unrelated refactors and hidden policy changes?¥n¥n## Output Contract¥n¥nFor findings:¥n¥n- `SEVERITY`¥n- `LOCATION`¥n- `ISSUE`¥n- `WHY IT MATTERS`¥n- `REQUIRED FIX`¥n¥nFor a clean review:¥n¥n- `VERDICT: PASS`¥n- `EVIDENCE CHECKED`¥n- `TEST GAPS`¥n- `RESIDUAL RISK`
description: Findings-first code and workflow review with severity and blast-radius checks.
name: workbench-code-review

content: # Workbench Frontend Design QA¥n¥nUse this skill for frontend UI, desktop/web surfaces, screenshots, responsive checks, interaction polish, and design-system consistency.¥n¥n## QA Standard¥n¥n- Test the actual screen or rendered artifact, not just source code.¥n- Check desktop and mobile or narrow-width behavior when the UI is responsive.¥n- Verify text fit, no incoherent overlap, stable spacing, focus states, hover states, loading states, empty states, and error states.¥n- For operational tools, prefer dense, scannable, predictable layouts over marketing-style pages.¥n- Use real assets or rendered UI evidence when visual truth matters.¥n¥n## Design Checks¥n¥n- Information hierarchy is clear at first glance.¥n- Primary action is obvious and not visually drowned by decoration.¥n- Controls match their job: icon buttons for tools, toggles for binary state, tabs for views, menus for option sets.¥n- Cards are not nested inside cards unless the local design system already does that.¥n- Colors are not a one-note palette and contrast is readable.¥n- Motion or animation does not hide state or slow repeated work.¥n¥n## Output Contract¥n¥nReturn:¥n¥n- `DESIGN_VERDICT`: PASS / FLAG / BLOCK.¥n- `SURFACES CHECKED`: URL, file, viewport, screenshot, or app surface.¥n- `FINDINGS`: severity, location, and fix.¥n- `EVIDENCE`: screenshot path, browser check, or command output.¥n- `NEXT ACTION`: smallest design or implementation correction.
description: Frontend and visual QA discipline for responsive workbench-facing interfaces.
name: workbench-frontend-design-qa

content: # Workbench Review QA¥n¥nUse this skill for code review, workflow review, QA verification, release checks, and issue closeout.¥n¥n## Verdicts¥n¥n- `PASS`: objective is met and evidence is sufficient.¥n- `FLAG`: useful progress, but a non-blocking issue or missing evidence remains.¥n- `BLOCK`: objective is not met, unsafe, unverifiable, or materially wrong.¥n¥n## Review Method¥n¥n1. Identify the promised objective.¥n2. Inspect the real output, diff, issue comments, runtime state, or checked-out repo.¥n3. Compare evidence against the objective.¥n4. Verify the smallest real path that proves the claim.¥n5. Report findings first, ordered by severity.¥n6. Check command syntax and live-resource ownership when the work involves Multica CLI mutations.¥n7. If duplicate comments or artifacts exist, identify the primary artifact and explain why.¥n¥n## Findings Format¥n¥nFor problems, return:¥n¥n- severity,¥n- location or evidence,¥n- why it matters,¥n- required fix or next verification.¥n¥nFor clean reviews, return:¥n¥n- verdict,¥n- evidence checked,¥n- residual risk or test gap.¥n¥n## QA Rules¥n¥n- Do not accept `done` based on paraphrase alone.¥n- Distinguish content failures from workflow/tooling failures.¥n- If Multica workspace repo access is needed, use the workspace repository checkout path and report the commit/branch inspected.¥n- Keep evidence concise and reproducible.¥n- Verify `Workbench Max` remains untouched when a task says it must be preserved.
description: PASS FLAG BLOCK review and QA verification protocol.
name: workbench-review-qa
